titleStmt.title: Translation of NLS Adv. MS 15.1.18, no. 61

p.seg: To all to who shall view or hear this written deed Richenda, daughter of Humphrey of Berkeley, wishes eternal salvation in the Lord.

that I, by my own lawful authority, and of my own free will, from my own goodwill and by the consent of our lords the abbot and religious community of Arbroath, have given, granted, and by this my charter made firm, to Roger Wyrfaut the whole land that belonged to Hugh son of Waldef in the territory of Conveth; For him and his attorneys to hold of me and my heirs in feu and heritage, freely and quietly by its right marches, with common pasture at Scotston, with merchets and blodwites, and with all its other rightful pertinents and easements, free and quit from all service pertaining to me or my heirs, saving the forinsec service of the lord King, for which he and his attorneys shall answer, namely for as much as pertains to half a davach, and saving to myself my multure from the land that belongs to the mill of Conveth; which I shall not make more freely to Humphrey of Middleton and John Wishart [the ‘te’ which the scribe wrote here does not appear to make sense: a different version of this charter was copied into the St Andrews cartulary, St A. Lib. 285-6]; By rendering annually thereupon to me and my heirs, he and his attorneys, one pound of pepper at Pentecost when they shall come to the aforesaid land. And so that I or my heirs are not able to act against this donation, I have given an oath upon holy relics for myself and my heirs, I and my heirs submitting to the jurisdiction of the lord bishop of St Andrews or the archdeacon of the same place, whoever it should be at the time, so that either of them might by whatsoever means they may wish compel me and my heirs by censure to the observance of this donation. Not withstanding the exception of a court, or any other exception or appeal, or any remedy of civil or canon law.
